item.title: Ensuring quality in AI healthcare technologies
item.description: A project co-funded by the Turing has developed the first international guidelines for clinical trials involving AI

item.title: Optimising the world’s first underground farm
item.description: In the heart of London there is a farm like no other. It’s subterranean, sustainable and energy smart. It also has a digital twin looking out for its every need

item.title: Anti-bias test implemented by Amazon
item.description: A test for detecting bias in AI systems, created by Turing researchers, has been adopted by Amazon Web Services

item.title: Improving lives in Bradford
item.description: A collaboration between the Turing and Leeds Institute for Data Analytics is using data science to generate targeted policies for Bradford’s most deprived areas

item.title: Providing COVID-19 expertise to the UK government
item.description: The Turing-RSS Health Data Lab delivered invaluable insights to the UK Health Security Agency throughout the pandemic

item.title: Reducing the carbon footprint of the shipping industry
item.description: An algorithm that lowers ships’ greenhouse gas emissions by planning the most fuel-efficient route through the waves

item.title: Open minds: How open-source tools are broadening the horizons for data science
item.description: From understanding plankton to upgrading planes, the Turing is helping to spread the benefits of open AI and data science

item.title: Better together: The people-centred approaches driving forward data ethics
item.description: Participatory projects at the Turing are leading the way for responsible research and innovation in data science

item.title: Twinning ways: how digital twins will change our world for the better
item.description: Turing researchers are creating virtual bridges, farms and truck fleets to benefit the real-world counterparts

item.title: Developing a more accurate measure of the nation’s health
item.description: Working with the Office for National Statistics to carry out an in-depth statistical analysis of its Health Index

item.title: Revealing hidden details of the ocean floor
item.description: An algorithm that automatically detects and classifies marine creatures called sea pens

item.title: Demoing a digital twin of the UK energy system
item.description: Building towards a tool that will allow the government to predict the long-term impact of its energy policies

item.title: Understanding online abuse of Premier League footballers
item.description: The Online Harms Observatory uses machine learning to detect toxic content on social media

item.title: Powering population models with synthetic data
item.description: A flexible tool that can provide data for research spanning health, climate, transport and more

item.title: Enabling researchers to work safely with sensitive data
item.description: Our open-source project is making it easier for other organisations to create secure computing environments

item.title: Mapping habitats in the Peak District National Park
item.description: An AI-powered tool is guiding wildlife monitoring and landscape restoration

item.title: Demonstrating the power of AI in land use planning
item.description: Partnering with the Geospatial Commission to simulate the effects of planning decisions on residents’ quality of life

item.title: Helping governments to develop policies that make a difference
item.description: Allowing policy makers to estimate the outcomes of their decisions in a holistic way
